fault_hypothesis: Line 73 uses a strict greater-than comparison (`> limit`) instead of `>= limit`, so when `len(recent) == limit` the function incorrectly returns `True` (allowed) with `remaining == 0` instead of `False, 0`.

constraints.intent: When recent count equals the limit, the condition must be true so the function returns False,0 — this is violated by the strict > instead of >=.
In the else branch (allowed=True), remaining must be strictly positive; if len(recent)==limit, remaining would be 0 which contradicts allowing the message.
Sanity check that the comparison covers the equality case — the bug is that equality is not handled by the > operator.
Verify recent is a valid subset of timestamps before the comparison is made.
The function should deny (return False) whenever recent count is at or above the limit, catching the off-by-one in the comparison.
